revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has English label - based on Wikidata English label
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11405036]] Wikidata name: Toichi district [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Pathnavbox| *{{Pathnav|List of provinces of Japan|Kinai|Yamato Province}} *{{Pathnav|Japan|Kinki Region|Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Nara Toichi-gun.png|frame|The extent of Toichi District in Nara Prefecture]] '''Toichi District''' (十市郡, Tōichi-gun<ref>From ancient to medieval times, it was also read as "Tōchi-gun" (角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県).</ref>) was a [[:en:District|District]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] (formerly [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
== District Area ==
The area of the district when it was established as an administrative division in [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[Meiji]] 13) corresponds to the following regions:
* Parts of [[:en:Kashihara|Kashihara]], including Iitaka, Ōgaki, Toyoda, Niiguchi, Kamishōji, Uchizen, Kitayagi, Kihara, Yamanobō, Ishiharada, Deai, Kanzai, Shimohachitsuri, Kinomoto, and northeastern areas<ref>Exact details on [[address notation|residential address implementation]] in Yagi City remain unclear.</ref>. * Parts of [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], including Arashiki, Higashishindō, Ōfuku, Kibi, Abe, Tani, Sakurai, Asako, Kurahashi, Kudario, and Awabara. * Parts of [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]], including Matsumoto, Nishitakeda, Jūroku-men, Hotsu, unnamed areas, Chiyo, and Aji-ma, southwest of these places.
== History ==
=== Ancient Period ===
==== Villages ====
The [[Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists the following villages in the district: * Iitomi * Kawabe (加八乃倍) * Ikegami * Kanbe
==== Shikinaisha (Ceremonial Shrines) ====
The [[Engishiki]] lists the following {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}}: {{Shikinaisha header}} {{Shikinaisha list for Yamato Province}} {{Shikinaisha footer}}
=== Early Modern Period ===
According to the [[Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō]] (a record of early Meiji-era holdings), the following statuses were in place under the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] administration and feudal lords. Lands managed directly by the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] were under the jurisdiction of the [[Nara Bugyō]], with some containing [[temple lands]] (●) or [[exempt temple territories]] (○). There were three towns and 81 villages in total.
[Summary of holdings table and specific details provided.]
=== Modern Period ===
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868): ** February 5 - [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] territories transferred to the jurisdiction of the '''[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'''. ** May 21 - Flag-holder territories and temple lands fall under Nara Prefecture. ** July 29 - Nara Prefecture renamed. * Meiji 4 (1871): ** July 14 - [[Abolition of the Han System]] leads to territorial divisions managed by [[Tsu Prefecture]], [[Kōriyama Prefecture]], [[Hisai Prefecture]], [[Tawaramoto Prefecture]], and [[Takatori Prefecture]]. ** November 22 - Administrative consolidation places the entire region under Nara Prefecture. * Meiji 13 (1880): ** April 15 - Toichi District is established under the [[Gun-ku-chōson System]]. * Meiji 30 (1897): ** April 1 - [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[Toichi District]], and [[:en:Uda District|Uda District]] are merged to form the new [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], leading to the abolition of Toichi District.
== Administration ==
; District Officials [Details of district officials across Nara, Osaka, and administrative transitions provided.]
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor="Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten" Editorial Committee|year=1990|title=[[Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|volume=29 Nara Prefecture|isbn=4040012909}} * [https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/up-cgi/login.pl?p=param/kyud/db_param Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō Database] == See Also == * [[List of defunct districts of Japan]]
{{s-start}} {{s-bef|before=-----|title=Transition of Administrative Divisions|years= - 1897}} {{s-ttl|title=Toichi District}} {{s-aft|after=[[Shiki District}} {{end}} {{Kinai Five Provinces Districts}}
{{translated page|ja|十市郡|version=102374891|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]]
}}
[[ja:十市郡]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11405036]] Wikidata name: Toichi district [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Pathnavbox| *{{Pathnav|List of provinces of Japan|Kinai|Yamato Province}} *{{Pathnav|Japan|Kinki Region|Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Nara Toichi-gun.png|frame|The extent of Toichi District in Nara Prefecture]] '''Toichi District''' (十市郡, Tōichi-gun<ref>From ancient to medieval times, it was also read as "Tōchi-gun" (角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県).</ref>) was a [[:en:District|District]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] (formerly [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
== District Area ==
The area of the district when it was established as an administrative division in [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[Meiji]] 13) corresponds to the following regions:
* Parts of [[:en:Kashihara|Kashihara]], including Iitaka, Ōgaki, Toyoda, Niiguchi, Kamishōji, Uchizen, Kitayagi, Kihara, Yamanobō, Ishiharada, Deai, Kanzai, Shimohachitsuri, Kinomoto, and northeastern areas<ref>Exact details on [[address notation|residential address implementation]] in Yagi City remain unclear.</ref>. * Parts of [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], including Arashiki, Higashishindō, Ōfuku, Kibi, Abe, Tani, Sakurai, Asako, Kurahashi, Kudario, and Awabara. * Parts of [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]], including Matsumoto, Nishitakeda, Jūroku-men, Hotsu, unnamed areas, Chiyo, and Aji-ma, southwest of these places.
== History ==
=== Ancient Period ===
==== Villages ====
The [[Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists the following villages in the district: * Iitomi * Kawabe (加八乃倍) * Ikegami * Kanbe
==== Shikinaisha (Ceremonial Shrines) ====
The [[Engishiki]] lists the following {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}}: {{Shikinaisha header}} {{Shikinaisha list for Yamato Province}} {{Shikinaisha footer}}
=== Early Modern Period ===
According to the [[Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō]] (a record of early Meiji-era holdings), the following statuses were in place under the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] administration and feudal lords. Lands managed directly by the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] were under the jurisdiction of the [[Nara Bugyō]], with some containing [[temple lands]] (●) or [[exempt temple territories]] (○). There were three towns and 81 villages in total.
[Summary of holdings table and specific details provided.]
=== Modern Period ===
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868): ** February 5 - [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] territories transferred to the jurisdiction of the '''[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'''. ** May 21 - Flag-holder territories and temple lands fall under Nara Prefecture. ** July 29 - Nara Prefecture renamed. * Meiji 4 (1871): ** July 14 - [[Abolition of the Han System]] leads to territorial divisions managed by [[Tsu Prefecture]], [[Kōriyama Prefecture]], [[Hisai Prefecture]], [[Tawaramoto Prefecture]], and [[Takatori Prefecture]]. ** November 22 - Administrative consolidation places the entire region under Nara Prefecture. * Meiji 13 (1880): ** April 15 - Toichi District is established under the [[Gun-ku-chōson System]]. * Meiji 30 (1897): ** April 1 - [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[Toichi District]], and [[:en:Uda District|Uda District]] are merged to form the new [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], leading to the abolition of Toichi District.
== Administration ==
; District Officials [Details of district officials across Nara, Osaka, and administrative transitions provided.]
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor="Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten" Editorial Committee|year=1990|title=[[Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|volume=29 Nara Prefecture|isbn=4040012909}} * [https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/up-cgi/login.pl?p=param/kyud/db_param Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō Database] == See Also == * [[List of defunct districts of Japan]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]" to ""
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11405036]] Wikidata name: Toichi district [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Pathnavbox| *{{Pathnav|List of provinces of Japan|Kinai|Yamato Province}} *{{Pathnav|Japan|Kinki Region|Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Nara Toichi-gun.png|frame|The extent of Toichi District in Nara Prefecture]] '''Toichi District''' (十市郡, Tōichi-gun<ref>From ancient to medieval times, it was also read as "Tōchi-gun" (角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県).</ref>) was a [[:en:District|District]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] (formerly [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
== District Area ==
The area of the district when it was established as an administrative division in [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[Meiji]] 13) corresponds to the following regions:
* Parts of [[:en:Kashihara|Kashihara]], including Iitaka, Ōgaki, Toyoda, Niiguchi, Kamishōji, Uchizen, Kitayagi, Kihara, Yamanobō, Ishiharada, Deai, Kanzai, Shimohachitsuri, Kinomoto, and northeastern areas<ref>Exact details on [[address notation|residential address implementation]] in Yagi City remain unclear.</ref>. * Parts of [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], including Arashiki, Higashishindō, Ōfuku, Kibi, Abe, Tani, Sakurai, Asako, Kurahashi, Kudario, and Awabara. * Parts of [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]], including Matsumoto, Nishitakeda, Jūroku-men, Hotsu, unnamed areas, Chiyo, and Aji-ma, southwest of these places.
== History ==
=== Ancient Period ===
==== Villages ====
The [[Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists the following villages in the district: * Iitomi * Kawabe (加八乃倍) * Ikegami * Kanbe
==== Shikinaisha (Ceremonial Shrines) ====
The [[Engishiki]] lists the following {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}}: {{Shikinaisha header}} {{Shikinaisha list for Yamato Province}} {{Shikinaisha footer}}
=== Early Modern Period ===
According to the [[Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō]] (a record of early Meiji-era holdings), the following statuses were in place under the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] administration and feudal lords. Lands managed directly by the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] were under the jurisdiction of the [[Nara Bugyō]], with some containing [[temple lands]] (●) or [[exempt temple territories]] (○). There were three towns and 81 villages in total.
[Summary of holdings table and specific details provided.]
=== Modern Period ===
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868): ** February 5 - [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] territories transferred to the jurisdiction of the '''[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'''. ** May 21 - Flag-holder territories and temple lands fall under Nara Prefecture. ** July 29 - Nara Prefecture renamed. * Meiji 4 (1871): ** July 14 - [[Abolition of the Han System]] leads to territorial divisions managed by [[Tsu Prefecture]], [[Kōriyama Prefecture]], [[Hisai Prefecture]], [[Tawaramoto Prefecture]], and [[Takatori Prefecture]]. ** November 22 - Administrative consolidation places the entire region under Nara Prefecture. * Meiji 13 (1880): ** April 15 - Toichi District is established under the [[Gun-ku-chōson System]]. * Meiji 30 (1897): ** April 1 - [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[Toichi District]], and [[:en:Uda District|Uda District]] are merged to form the new [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], leading to the abolition of Toichi District.
== Administration ==
; District Officials [Details of district officials across Nara, Osaka, and administrative transitions provided.]
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor="Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten" Editorial Committee|year=1990|title=[[Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|volume=29 Nara Prefecture|isbn=4040012909}} * [https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/up-cgi/login.pl?p=param/kyud/db_param Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō Database] == See Also == * [[List of defunct districts of Japan]]
{{s-start}} {{s-bef|before=-----|title=Transition of Administrative Divisions|years= - 1897}} {{s-ttl|title=Toichi District}} {{s-aft|after=[[Shiki District}} {{end}} {{Kinai Five Provinces Districts}}
{{translated page|ja|十市郡|version=102374891|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11405036]] Wikidata name: Toichi district [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Pathnavbox| *{{Pathnav|List of provinces of Japan|Kinai|Yamato Province}} *{{Pathnav|Japan|Kinki Region|Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Nara Toichi-gun.png|frame|The extent of Toichi District in Nara Prefecture]] '''Toichi District''' (十市郡, Tōichi-gun<ref>From ancient to medieval times, it was also read as "Tōchi-gun" (角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県).</ref>) was a [[:en:District|District]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] (formerly [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
== District Area ==
The area of the district when it was established as an administrative division in [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[Meiji]] 13) corresponds to the following regions:
* Parts of [[:en:Kashihara|Kashihara]], including Iitaka, Ōgaki, Toyoda, Niiguchi, Kamishōji, Uchizen, Kitayagi, Kihara, Yamanobō, Ishiharada, Deai, Kanzai, Shimohachitsuri, Kinomoto, and northeastern areas<ref>Exact details on [[address notation|residential address implementation]] in Yagi City remain unclear.</ref>. * Parts of [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], including Arashiki, Higashishindō, Ōfuku, Kibi, Abe, Tani, Sakurai, Asako, Kurahashi, Kudario, and Awabara. * Parts of [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]], including Matsumoto, Nishitakeda, Jūroku-men, Hotsu, unnamed areas, Chiyo, and Aji-ma, southwest of these places.
== History ==
=== Ancient Period ===
==== Villages ====
The [[Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists the following villages in the district: * Iitomi * Kawabe (加八乃倍) * Ikegami * Kanbe
==== Shikinaisha (Ceremonial Shrines) ====
The [[Engishiki]] lists the following {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}}: {{Shikinaisha header}} {{Shikinaisha list for Yamato Province}} {{Shikinaisha footer}}
=== Early Modern Period ===
According to the [[Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō]] (a record of early Meiji-era holdings), the following statuses were in place under the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] administration and feudal lords. Lands managed directly by the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] were under the jurisdiction of the [[Nara Bugyō]], with some containing [[temple lands]] (●) or [[exempt temple territories]] (○). There were three towns and 81 villages in total.
[Summary of holdings table and specific details provided.]
=== Modern Period ===
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868): ** February 5 - [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] territories transferred to the jurisdiction of the '''[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'''. ** May 21 - Flag-holder territories and temple lands fall under Nara Prefecture. ** July 29 - Nara Prefecture renamed. * Meiji 4 (1871): ** July 14 - [[Abolition of the Han System]] leads to territorial divisions managed by [[Tsu Prefecture]], [[Kōriyama Prefecture]], [[Hisai Prefecture]], [[Tawaramoto Prefecture]], and [[Takatori Prefecture]]. ** November 22 - Administrative consolidation places the entire region under Nara Prefecture. * Meiji 13 (1880): ** April 15 - Toichi District is established under the [[Gun-ku-chōson System]]. * Meiji 30 (1897): ** April 1 - [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[Toichi District]], and [[:en:Uda District|Uda District]] are merged to form the new [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], leading to the abolition of Toichi District.
== Administration ==
; District Officials [Details of district officials across Nara, Osaka, and administrative transitions provided.]
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor="Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten" Editorial Committee|year=1990|title=[[Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|volume=29 Nara Prefecture|isbn=4040012909}} * [https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/up-cgi/login.pl?p=param/kyud/db_param Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō Database] == See Also == * [[List of defunct districts of Japan]]

Lands managed directly by the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] were under the jurisdiction of the [[Nara Bugyō]], with some containing [[temple lands]] (●) or [[exempt temple territories]] (○). There were three towns and 81 villages in total.
[Summary of holdings table and specific details provided.]
=== Modern Period ===
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868): ** February 5 - [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] territories transferred to the jurisdiction of the '''[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'''. ** May 21 - Flag-holder territories and temple lands fall under Nara Prefecture. ** July 29 - Nara Prefecture renamed. * Meiji 4 (1871): ** July 14 - [[Abolition of the Han System]] leads to territorial divisions managed by [[Tsu Prefecture]], [[Kōriyama Prefecture]], [[Hisai Prefecture]], [[Tawaramoto Prefecture]], and [[Takatori Prefecture]]. ** November 22 - Administrative consolidation places the entire region under Nara Prefecture. * Meiji 13 (1880): ** April 15 - Toichi District is established under the [[Gun-ku-chōson System]]. * Meiji 30 (1897): ** April 1 - [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[Toichi District]], and [[:en:Uda District|Uda District]] are merged to form the new [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], leading to the abolition of Toichi District.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11405036]] Wikidata name: Toichi district [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Pathnavbox| *{{Pathnav|List of provinces of Japan|Kinai|Yamato Province}} *{{Pathnav|Japan|Kinki Region|Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Nara Toichi-gun.png|frame|The extent of Toichi District in Nara Prefecture]] '''Toichi District''' (十市郡, Tōichi-gun<ref>From ancient to medieval times, it was also read as "Tōchi-gun" (角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県).</ref>) was a [[:en:District|District]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] (formerly [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
== District Area ==
The area of the district when it was established as an administrative division in [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[Meiji]] 13) corresponds to the following regions:
* Parts of [[:en:Kashihara|Kashihara]], including Iitaka, Ōgaki, Toyoda, Niiguchi, Kamishōji, Uchizen, Kitayagi, Kihara, Yamanobō, Ishiharada, Deai, Kanzai, Shimohachitsuri, Kinomoto, and northeastern areas<ref>Exact details on [[address notation|residential address implementation]] in Yagi City remain unclear.</ref>. * Parts of [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], including Arashiki, Higashishindō, Ōfuku, Kibi, Abe, Tani, Sakurai, Asako, Kurahashi, Kudario, and Awabara. * Parts of [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]], including Matsumoto, Nishitakeda, Jūroku-men, Hotsu, unnamed areas, Chiyo, and Aji-ma, southwest of these places.
== History ==
=== Ancient Period ===
==== Villages ====
The [[Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists the following villages in the district: * Iitomi * Kawabe (加八乃倍) * Ikegami * Kanbe
==== Shikinaisha (Ceremonial Shrines) ====
The [[Engishiki]] lists the following {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}}: {{Shikinaisha header}} {{Shikinaisha list for Yamato Province}} {{Shikinaisha footer}}

The [[Engishiki]] lists the following {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}}: {{Shikinaisha header}} {{Shikinaisha list for Yamato Province}} {{Shikinaisha footer}}
=== Early Modern Period ===
According to the [[Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō]] (a record of early Meiji-era holdings), the following statuses were in place under the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] administration and feudal lords. Lands managed directly by the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] were under the jurisdiction of the [[Nara Bugyō]], with some containing [[temple lands]] (●) or [[exempt temple territories]] (○). There were three towns and 81 villages in total.
[Summary of holdings table and specific details provided.]
=== Modern Period ===
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868): ** February 5 - [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] territories transferred to the jurisdiction of the '''[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'''. ** May 21 - Flag-holder territories and temple lands fall under Nara Prefecture. ** July 29 - Nara Prefecture renamed. * Meiji 4 (1871): ** July 14 - [[Abolition of the Han System]] leads to territorial divisions managed by [[Tsu Prefecture]], [[Kōriyama Prefecture]], [[Hisai Prefecture]], [[Tawaramoto Prefecture]], and [[Takatori Prefecture]]. ** November 22 - Administrative consolidation places the entire region under Nara Prefecture. * Meiji 13 (1880): ** April 15 - Toichi District is established under the [[Gun-ku-chōson System]]. * Meiji 30 (1897): ** April 1 - [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[Toichi District]], and [[:en:Uda District|Uda District]] are merged to form the new [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], leading to the abolition of Toichi District.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11405036]] Wikidata name: Toichi district [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Pathnavbox| *{{Pathnav|List of provinces of Japan|Kinai|Yamato Province}} *{{Pathnav|Japan|Kinki Region|Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Nara Toichi-gun.png|frame|The extent of Toichi District in Nara Prefecture]] '''Toichi District''' (十市郡, Tōichi-gun<ref>From ancient to medieval times, it was also read as "Tōchi-gun" (角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県).</ref>) was a [[:en:District|District]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] (formerly [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
== District Area ==
The area of the district when it was established as an administrative division in [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[Meiji]] 13) corresponds to the following regions:
* Parts of [[:en:Kashihara|Kashihara]], including Iitaka, Ōgaki, Toyoda, Niiguchi, Kamishōji, Uchizen, Kitayagi, Kihara, Yamanobō, Ishiharada, Deai, Kanzai, Shimohachitsuri, Kinomoto, and northeastern areas<ref>Exact details on [[address notation|residential address implementation]] in Yagi City remain unclear.</ref>. * Parts of [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], including Arashiki, Higashishindō, Ōfuku, Kibi, Abe, Tani, Sakurai, Asako, Kurahashi, Kudario, and Awabara. * Parts of [[:en:Shiki District|Shiki District]], [[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]], including Matsumoto, Nishitakeda, Jūroku-men, Hotsu, unnamed areas, Chiyo, and Aji-ma, southwest of these places.

The [[Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists the following villages in the district: * Iitomi * Kawabe (加八乃倍) * Ikegami * Kanbe

== District Area ==
The area of the district when it was established as an administrative division in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} ([[Meiji]] 13) corresponds to the following regions:
* Parts of [[:en:Kashihara|Kashihara]], including Iitaka, Ōgaki, Toyoda, Niiguchi, Kamishōji, Uchizen, Kitayagi, Kihara, Yamanobō, Ishiharada, Deai, Kanzai, Shimohachitsuri, Kinomoto, and northeastern areas<ref>Exact details on [[address notation|residential address implementation]] in Yagi City remain unclear.</ref>. * Parts of {{ill|Sakurai|en|Sakurai|WD=Q420575|lt=Sakurai}}, including Arashiki, Higashishindō, Ōfuku, Kibi, Abe, Tani, Sakurai, Asako, Kurahashi, Kudario, and Awabara. * Parts of {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District}}, {{ill|Tawaramoto, Nara|en|Tawaramoto, Nara|WD=Q1203159|lt=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, including Matsumoto, Nishitakeda, Jūroku-men, Hotsu, unnamed areas, Chiyo, and Aji-ma, southwest of these places.
== History ==
=== Ancient Period ===
==== Villages ====
The [[Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists the following villages in the district: * Iitomi * Kawabe (加八乃倍) * Ikegami * Kanbe
==== Shikinaisha (Ceremonial Shrines) ====
The [[Engishiki]] lists the following {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}}: {{Shikinaisha header}} {{Shikinaisha list for Yamato Province}} {{Shikinaisha footer}}
=== Early Modern Period ===
According to the [[Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō]] (a record of early Meiji-era holdings), the following statuses were in place under the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] administration and feudal lords. Lands managed directly by the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} were under the jurisdiction of the [[Nara Bugyō]], with some containing [[temple lands]] (●) or [[exempt temple territories]] (○). There were three towns and 81 villages in total.
[Summary of holdings table and specific details provided.]
=== Modern Period ===
* {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4 (1868): ** February 5 - {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} territories transferred to the jurisdiction of the '''{{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}'''. ** May 21 - Flag-holder territories and temple lands fall under Nara Prefecture. ** July 29 - Nara Prefecture renamed. * Meiji 4 (1871): ** July 14 - [[Abolition of the Han System]] leads to territorial divisions managed by [[Tsu Prefecture]], [[Kōriyama Prefecture]], [[Hisai Prefecture]], [[Tawaramoto Prefecture]], and [[Takatori Prefecture]]. ** November 22 - Administrative consolidation places the entire region under Nara Prefecture. * Meiji 13 (1880): ** April 15 - Toichi District is established under the [[Gun-ku-chōson System]]. * Meiji 30 (1897): ** April 1 - {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District}}, [[Toichi District]], and {{ill|Uda District|en|Uda District|WD=Q113112004|lt=Uda District}} are merged to form the new {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District}}, leading to the abolition of Toichi District.
== Administration ==
; District Officials [Details of district officials across Nara, Osaka, and administrative transitions provided.]
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor="Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten" Editorial Committee|year=1990|title=[[Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|volume=29 Nara Prefecture|isbn=4040012909}} * [https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/up-cgi/login.pl?p=param/kyud/db_param Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō Database] == See Also == * [[List of defunct districts of Japan]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十市郡]][[zh:十市郡]]
{{Pathnavbox| *{{Pathnav|List of provinces of Japan|Kinai|Yamato Province}} *{{Pathnav|Japan|Kinki Region|Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Nara Toichi-gun.png|frame|The extent of Toichi District in Nara Prefecture]] '''Toichi District''' (十市郡, Tōichi-gun<ref>From ancient to medieval times, it was also read as "Tōchi-gun" (角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県).</ref>) was a {{ill|District|en|District|WD=Q149621|lt=District|1=District}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} (formerly {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}).
== District Area ==
The area of the district when it was established as an administrative division in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880|1=1880}} ([[Meiji]] 13) corresponds to the following regions:
* Parts of [[:en:Kashihara|Kashihara]], including Iitaka, Ōgaki, Toyoda, Niiguchi, Kamishōji, Uchizen, Kitayagi, Kihara, Yamanobō, Ishiharada, Deai, Kanzai, Shimohachitsuri, Kinomoto, and northeastern areas<ref>Exact details on [[address notation|residential address implementation]] in Yagi City remain unclear.</ref>. * Parts of {{ill|Sakurai|en|Sakurai|WD=Q420575|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai}}, including Arashiki, Higashishindō, Ōfuku, Kibi, Abe, Tani, Sakurai, Asako, Kurahashi, Kudario, and Awabara. * Parts of {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District}}, {{ill|Tawaramoto, Nara|en|Tawaramoto, Nara|WD=Q1203159|lt=Tawaramoto, Nara|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, including Matsumoto, Nishitakeda, Jūroku-men, Hotsu, unnamed areas, Chiyo, and Aji-ma, southwest of these places.
== History ==
=== Ancient Period ===
==== Villages ====
The [[Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists the following villages in the district: * Iitomi * Kawabe (加八乃倍) * Ikegami * Kanbe
==== Shikinaisha (Ceremonial Shrines) ====
The [[Engishiki]] lists the following {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}}: {{Shikinaisha header}} {{Shikinaisha list for Yamato Province}} {{Shikinaisha footer}}
=== Early Modern Period ===
According to the [[Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō]] (a record of early Meiji-era holdings), the following statuses were in place under the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] administration and feudal lords. Lands managed directly by the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} were under the jurisdiction of the [[Nara Bugyō]], with some containing [[temple lands]] (●) or [[exempt temple territories]] (○). There were three towns and 81 villages in total.
[Summary of holdings table and specific details provided.]
=== Modern Period ===
* {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 4 (1868): ** February 5 - {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} territories transferred to the jurisdiction of the '''{{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}'''. ** May 21 - Flag-holder territories and temple lands fall under Nara Prefecture. ** July 29 - Nara Prefecture renamed. * Meiji 4 (1871): ** July 14 - [[Abolition of the Han System]] leads to territorial divisions managed by [[Tsu Prefecture]], [[Kōriyama Prefecture]], [[Hisai Prefecture]], [[Tawaramoto Prefecture]], and [[Takatori Prefecture]]. ** November 22 - Administrative consolidation places the entire region under Nara Prefecture. * Meiji 13 (1880): ** April 15 - Toichi District is established under the [[Gun-ku-chōson System]]. * Meiji 30 (1897): ** April 1 - {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District}}, [[Toichi District]], and {{ill|Uda District|en|Uda District|WD=Q113112004|lt=Uda District|1=Uda District}} are merged to form the new {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District}}, leading to the abolition of Toichi District.
== Administration ==
; District Officials [Details of district officials across Nara, Osaka, and administrative transitions provided.]
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor="Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten" Editorial Committee|year=1990|title=[[Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|volume=29 Nara Prefecture|isbn=4040012909}} * [https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/up-cgi/login.pl?p=param/kyud/db_param Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō Database] == See Also == * [[List of defunct districts of Japan]]

{{translated page|ja|十市郡|version=102374891|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The [[Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists the following villages in the district: * Iitomi * Kawabe (加八乃倍) * Ikegami * Kanbe
==== Shikinaisha (Ceremonial Shrines) ====
The [[Engishiki]] lists the following {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}}: {{Shikinaisha header}} {{Shikinaisha list for Yamato Province}} {{Shikinaisha footer}}
=== Early Modern Period ===
According to the [[Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō]] (a record of early Meiji-era holdings), the following statuses were in place under the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] administration and feudal lords. Lands managed directly by the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} were under the jurisdiction of the [[Nara Bugyō]], with some containing [[temple lands]] (●) or [[exempt temple territories]] (○). There were three towns and 81 villages in total.
[Summary of holdings table and specific details provided.]
=== Modern Period ===
* {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 4 (1868): ** February 5 - {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} territories transferred to the jurisdiction of the '''{{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}'''. ** May 21 - Flag-holder territories and temple lands fall under Nara Prefecture. ** July 29 - Nara Prefecture renamed. * Meiji 4 (1871): ** July 14 - [[Abolition of the Han System]] leads to territorial divisions managed by [[Tsu Prefecture]], [[Kōriyama Prefecture]], [[Hisai Prefecture]], [[Tawaramoto Prefecture]], and [[Takatori Prefecture]]. ** November 22 - Administrative consolidation places the entire region under Nara Prefecture. * Meiji 13 (1880): ** April 15 - Toichi District is established under the [[Gun-ku-chōson System]]. * Meiji 30 (1897): ** April 1 - {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District}}, [[Toichi District]], and {{ill|Uda District|en|Uda District|WD=Q113112004|lt=Uda District|1=Uda District}} are merged to form the new {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District}}, leading to the abolition of Toichi District.
== Administration ==
; District Officials [Details of district officials across Nara, Osaka, and administrative transitions provided.]

revision.text:
{{Pathnavbox| *{{Pathnav|List of provinces of Japan|Kinai|Yamato Province}} *{{Pathnav|Japan|Kinki Region|Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Nara Toichi-gun.png|frame|The extent of Toichi District in Nara Prefecture]] '''Toichi District''' (十市郡, Tōichi-gun<ref>From ancient to medieval times, it was also read as "Tōchi-gun" (角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県).</ref>) was a {{ill|District|en|District|WD=Q149621|lt=District|1=District}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} (formerly {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}).
== District Area ==
The area of the district when it was established as an administrative division in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880|1=1880}} ([[Meiji]] 13) corresponds to the following regions:
* Parts of [[:en:Kashihara|Kashihara]], including Iitaka, Ōgaki, Toyoda, Niiguchi, Kamishōji, Uchizen, Kitayagi, Kihara, Yamanobō, Ishiharada, Deai, Kanzai, Shimohachitsuri, Kinomoto, and northeastern areas<ref>Exact details on [[address notation|residential address implementation]] in Yagi City remain unclear.</ref>. * Parts of {{ill|Sakurai|en|Sakurai|WD=Q420575|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai}}, including Arashiki, Higashishindō, Ōfuku, Kibi, Abe, Tani, Sakurai, Asako, Kurahashi, Kudario, and Awabara. * Parts of {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District}}, {{ill|Tawaramoto, Nara|en|Tawaramoto, Nara|WD=Q1203159|lt=Tawaramoto, Nara|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, including Matsumoto, Nishitakeda, Jūroku-men, Hotsu, unnamed areas, Chiyo, and Aji-ma, southwest of these places.
== History ==
=== Ancient Period ===
==== Villages ====
The [[Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists the following villages in the district: * Iitomi * Kawabe (加八乃倍) * Ikegami * Kanbe
==== Shikinaisha (Ceremonial Shrines) ====

=== Early Modern Period ===
According to the [[Kyū-taka Kyū-ryō Torishirabechō]] (a record of early Meiji-era holdings), the following statuses were in place under the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] administration and feudal lords. Lands managed directly by the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} were under the jurisdiction of the [[Nara Bugyō]], with some containing [[temple lands]] (●) or [[exempt temple territories]] (○). There were three towns and 81 villages in total.
[Summary of holdings table and specific details provided.]
=== Modern Period ===
* {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 4 (1868): ** February 5 - {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} territories transferred to the jurisdiction of the '''{{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}'''. ** May 21 - Flag-holder territories and temple lands fall under Nara Prefecture. ** July 29 - Nara Prefecture renamed. * Meiji 4 (1871): ** July 14 - [[Abolition of the Han System]] leads to territorial divisions managed by [[Tsu Prefecture]], [[Kōriyama Prefecture]], [[Hisai Prefecture]], [[Tawaramoto Prefecture]], and [[Takatori Prefecture]]. ** November 22 - Administrative consolidation places the entire region under Nara Prefecture. * Meiji 13 (1880): ** April 15 - Toichi District is established under the [[Gun-ku-chōson System]]. * Meiji 30 (1897): ** April 1 - {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District}}, [[Toichi District]], and {{ill|Uda District|en|Uda District|WD=Q113112004|lt=Uda District|1=Uda District}} are merged to form the new {{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|WD=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District}}, leading to the abolition of Toichi District.
